yesterdayAnalysis: Rest day executed perfectly on June 22nd, providing critical recovery after Friday's big Oregon course simulation (114 TSS) and Saturday's brick run. The complete day off allowed adaptation before this week's race-prep taper begins, and sleep quality rebounded to 80/100 with excellent deep sleep (110min) and normalized RHR (54bpm).
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned 2,500m pool swim with 4x100 at race pace to dial in Sunday's SDIT swim intensity. Keep the effort controlled but sharp — this is about rehearsing race feel, not breaking yourself 5 days out from competition.
weekProgress: Week 10 of 10 is on-plan at 42 TSS (14% of 300 target) with 5 days remaining including today. This low percentage is completely normal — the week is intentionally front-loaded with recovery (rest day yesterday) before Sunday's race, with only moderate swim sessions Tuesday/Thursday and minimal volume Friday/Saturday. No missed sessions.
keyInsight: This is a race week taper to SDIT on Sunday — the 300 TSS target includes race day itself, so training volume should stay light and sharp, not push toward arbitrary accumulated stress.
positives: Sleep rebounded strongly to 80/100 last night with 110min deep sleep after prior nights' recovery deficit
Monday's Eagleman ride executed perfectly at easy-moderate intensity (TSS 42, HR 124) for taper legs
Last week's Oregon full-course simulation delivered solid 114 TSS at controlled HR 138 — race execution dialed in
